evals.prompt: I just finished implementing a user notification feature. I've staged all changes. Write a good commit message for me.
evals.expected_output: Should read the staged diff first, then write a Conventional Commits formatted message with type, scope, subject, and body explaining why.
assertions: Runs git diff --staged to read the actual changes
Body explains WHY, not just WHAT
Checks if the staged changes are a single logical unit
If multiple logical changes, suggests splitting into separate commits

evals.prompt: I need to write a PR description for a branch that adds Stripe payment integration. The branch has 12 commits including some WIP and fix-typo commits. We're using a standard PR template.
evals.expected_output: Should read the branch diff and commit log, suggest squashing the WIP commits, then write a structured PR description.
assertions: Reads git log and diff for the branch
Suggests cleaning up the 'WIP' and 'fix-typo' commits via rebase/squash
Writes a complete PR description with What/Why/How/Testing sections
Links to the relevant issue or task
Includes a checklist with relevant items (especially security for payments)
Notes for reviewers highlights the payment-related security considerations

evals.prompt: We're a team of 4 starting a new project. What branching strategy should we use? We deploy to production multiple times a day.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend a simple strategy (main + short-lived feature branches) given small team and continuous deployment. Should NOT suggest GitFlow or other complex models.
assertions: Does NOT recommend GitFlow (too heavy for 4 devs + continuous deployment)
Suggests squash-merge as default merge strategy
Mentions PR review requirements appropriate for team size
